name: Schindler's List
reason: Steven Spielberg's devastating Holocaust epic about Oskar Schindler who saved over a thousand Jewish lives

name: The Godfather
reason: Francis Ford Coppola's landmark crime saga tracing the Corleone family's rise and fall

name: The Godfather Part II
reason: A rare sequel that surpasses the original, exploring both Vito's youth and Michael's descent

name: Titanic
reason: James Cameron's epic romance-disaster film that broke box office records and won 11 Oscars

name: The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King
reason: Peter Jackson's triumphant conclusion to the epic fantasy trilogy, winning all 11 nominated Oscars

name: Parasite
reason: Bong Joon-ho's genre-defying masterpiece about class divide that made history as first non-English Best Picture winner

name: Everything Everywhere All at Once
reason: The Daniels' multiverse-spanning martial arts comedy-drama that swept the Oscars with 7 wins

name: Oppenheimer
reason: Christopher Nolan's monumental biopic of the father of the atomic bomb

name: Paul Thomas Anderson - One Battle After Another
reason: For his sweeping political drama about an ex-revolutionary searching for his daughter, winning his first Oscar after 11 nominations

name: Birdman
reason: Alejandro Iñárritu's daring single-shot illusion comedy-drama about a washed-up actor

name: The Hurt Locker
reason: Kathryn Bigelow's tense war drama, making her the first woman to win Best Director

name: Steven Spielberg - Schindler's List
reason: For his profoundly humane direction of the Holocaust epic Schindler's List
